shared_properties: The lane overlay is authored for transparent compositing above the stage and below projectiles plus UI.
Core palette metadata remains primary #5C3317, background #2B1B17, and accent #D4AF37 while timing-support colors appear inside specs.colors_hex.
The reflect line geometry aligns with the player lane and preserves the fixed-screen duel composition.
differences: The asset draws a bright counter boundary, soft lane guide, and small sigil ticks that visually connect incoming spells to the player reaction point.
A cyan focal mark at the reflect boundary helps the player map the GDD timing rule to world space.
The lane keeps most detail horizontal so fast projectiles remain easy to read across the stage.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the duel loop, projectile attributes, UI layout, and Bevy runtime constraints for Reflect Academy.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the warm academy hall mood, layered duel composition, and luminous spell contrast.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load duel_lane_reflect_line.png during Loading and spawn it as a transparent overlay above the backdrop.
runtime_notes: Anchor the reflect boundary 20 px ahead of the player sprite left boundary so the counter window begins before projectile contact.
Keep the lane overlay static and let projectiles provide the motion cues.
Use alpha blending with no post blur so the line stays crisp under camera shake.

context.usage: Visible during combat to anchor projectile travel and communicate the counter boundary in world space.

layout_notes: Primary reflect boundary sits near x=420 within the lane overlay.
Horizontal guide line remains thinner than projectile silhouettes.
Sigil ticks occupy under 12% of the total overlay area.

acceptance_criteria: Canvas is exactly 1920x240 px with transparent pixels outside the lane art.
The reflect boundary remains readable over both dark floor and bright spell effects.
The overlay does not cover the card hand region at the bottom of the screen.
